skills.name: Internet Fundamentals
skills.description: Core concepts of how the internet works, including communication protocols, networking, and web architecture.
skills.notes: Covers HTTP/HTTPS, DNS resolution, domain names, hosting, browsers, and how data travels between client and server. Understanding these fundamentals is essential for debugging and designing backend systems.

skills.name: Backend Programming Language
skills.description: Languages used to build server-side logic, APIs, and scalable backend systems.
skills.notes: Popular choices include JavaScript (Node.js), Python, Java, Go, C#, and Rust. Focus on learning syntax, frameworks, concurrency, and memory handling relevant to backend development.

skills.name: Version Control Systems
skills.description: Tools for tracking code changes, collaboration, and managing project history.
skills.notes: Git is the industry standard, along with platforms like GitHub, GitLab, and Bitbucket. Learn branching strategies, pull requests, merging, and collaborative workflows.

skills.name: Databases
skills.description: Systems for storing, retrieving, and managing structured and unstructured data efficiently.
skills.notes: Includes relational databases (PostgreSQL, MySQL) and NoSQL databases (MongoDB, Redis). Learn indexing, transactions, normalization, scaling strategies, and query optimization.

skills.name: APIs (Application Programming Interfaces)
skills.description: Design and implementation of interfaces that allow communication between different software systems.
skills.notes: Covers REST, GraphQL, gRPC, and SOAP APIs. Learn request/response cycles, JSON handling, API versioning, documentation (OpenAPI), and best practices for building scalable services.

skills.name: Authentication & Security
skills.description: Techniques to secure backend systems, protect data, and manage user identity.
skills.notes: Includes JWT, OAuth, sessions, hashing (bcrypt), HTTPS, OWASP principles, and API security. Critical for preventing vulnerabilities like SQL injection, XSS, and CSRF.

skills.name: System Design & Architecture
skills.description: Designing scalable, reliable, and maintainable backend systems and infrastructure.
skills.notes: Covers microservices, monoliths, CI/CD, caching, load balancing, message queues (Kafka, RabbitMQ), containerization (Docker, Kubernetes), and observability. Essential for building production-grade systems.
